question:
Hello everyone,

I am trying to create a plugin for fabrication processes. At the moment, it is possible to correctly describe the processes thanks to a schema that consists of a modified version of the 'Process' class and a repeating class named 'FabricationProcessStep,' around which all classes that define a single step (such as PVD, CVD, Etching, etc.) are built. The SubSection steps are populated by varying the classes, and this part works fine.

However, the real issue arises when I try to develop an application for retrieving data from these processes. Specifically, I am unable to search for the quantities associated with the variable steps.

Is there a way to fix this issue?
answer: Hi there,  repeatable sub-sections can be accessed as lists. For example, if you want to access a quantity  ProcessStep.duration  composed inside a  Process , you can do so as  my_process.steps[0].duration If you give the process Step an identifier for the different Types you can use jmespath Syntax to Filter bei a specific id and then any quantity in the repeating subsection Or maybe Even Filter by m_def of the subsection but this I would have to Test https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/docs/howto/plugins/apps.html#column

question:
Hi, I have simulations run with the code FHI-AIMS. They are Molecular Dynamics and Single Point calculations. The Molecular Dynamics calculations include the Total Dipole Moment, which is not parsed in the data processing. 

The Single Point calculations calculate polarizabilities. But when uploaded the polarizability tensor is also not parsed. 

I would like to ask the developers to include  the parsing of the dipole moment and the polarizabilites? They are expensive calculations and necessary for the calculation of anharmonic IR and Raman spectra.

Let me know if you need further information or any help I can provide,
Thanks!?
answer:
Hi @Basillicus Adding support for these quantities should be no problem, however, it may take some time as we are currently overhauling NOMAD's simulation schema and parsers. My team and I will discuss this and get back to you by early next week I expect.

You mentioned also a problem with timing out during processing. Would you be able to add me as a reviewer to that upload? Or is the upload stuck in processing? @Basillicus Could you paste the link of whatever entries that you share with me here in the chat (only people with access will be able to open them). I have multiple accounts with many shared entries, so it is not easy for me to find otherwise.

Also, if this entry that you share is not an example with polarizabilities and dipole moments, could you also share one of those, so that I can look into extending our support. Thanks! Thank you for all of these links. 

For the time limit exceeded, there is no data in that upload. That means that there was an issue with the initial transfer of the .zip. This can occur randomly, especially for large files. I would just try the upload again and let me know if you have further issues. 

For the further discussion on polarizability and dipole moment support from FHIAims, I would like to continue the discuss here:  https://discordapp.com/channels/1201445470485106719/1336642604439310348/1336642604439310348

question:
Hi, I am trying to set up a nomad installation with a bare minimum and customized "Explore" menu. I have removed all plugins (except from pynxtools, which I need) from the requirements, however, I still see some default apps like "Calculations", "Materials", or "Eels" (see attachement). In About:Information I see that no related plugins are installed, yet these apps show up. In addition, the "All:Entries" App shows various categories, which produce errors when selecting.
How can I get rid of these default apps, and clean up the menu of the AllEnties App? 
Thanks for any help.?
answer:
Hi Laurenz, what version of NOMAD are you running? I believe Lauri fixed this issue in a recent update. I believe this was fixed here: https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/merge_requests/2239
But I'm not sure if we have made a release since then. I'll check. Indeed it seems like the latest release (1.3.13) is from November 29th and the fix by Lauri is from December 2nd. question:
I set up a nomad development installation on my local computer, but whenever I process an upload it directly gets stuck. In the GUI it says  Upload is processing… Pending: edit_upload_metadata 
The problem appears even without any entries in the upload, just when I want to change the name. The upload starts processing but never finishes. (Until I reset it via the CLI)
I have no idea how I can troubleshoot the problem. Maybe you can tell me where I should look for error messages.
In the terminal with the "uv run poe start" command it only says: 
 INFO:     127.0.0.1:50329 - "GET /uploads/bEM8TX2UTQi2AW5P0UguEQ/entries?page_size=5&page=1&order=asc&order_by=process_status HTTP/1.1" 200 OK 
which sounds okay. Where do you reckon I can find more information where the error occurs. Thanks!?
answer:
in your nomad.yaml you can try adjusting the console_log_level https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/docs/reference/config.html#services_1 I think it should be the terminal console This sounds like the worker has not correctly started. I would look at the docker logs and also check that the worker service has started correctly You can check the status of the services by running  docker ps . This will show "STATUS" column that you can check.

Also, you could inspect the live logs by removing the -d flag from the docker compose command:  docker compose up  instead of  docker compose up -d . This will show you all of the logs from all of the services. You could then copy/paste this whole log here and we can inspect them for any signs of possible failure.

question:
Hi, is there a way to make a search term widget in an app to show more then 5 hits when using the text input box?
Sometimes we have more results and its hard to find them by just typing... Changed the channel name: search widget showing more then 5 hits?
answer:
Suggestions are not available for custom quantities, but are collected for the shared quantities like entry_name, if so specified in their setup.

So:
Lab ID is a custom quantity => no suggestions
 entry_name  is one of the quantities shared across schemas, and it has been configured to collect suggestions => suggestions are shown There was an issue with the suggestions that was fixed recently in  develop . The fix is also available in 1.3.14 which is released today.

question:
I have a local test oasis running on my laptop and when I create a new upload and then reload the page it only says “Could not validate credentials. The given token is invalid. (401)“ and the upload page is not shown anymore.
This did not happen on my linux computer before, but I moved to a windows computer now and here the problem emerged. I changed nothing in the docker compose file.
Any spontaneous ideas what could be the problem here? Maybe someone of you has experienced this before?
answer:
No, no spontaneous ideas. We would need more insight, but I am afraid this will be really hard to troubleshoot remotely. This happens directly after clicking create upload? Or only after refreshing the browser window? What happens when you open the oasis in another tab? Does it persist? I get some weird timeout errors when working from home with the NOMAD keycloak which gives the same error. Usually waiting a minute and then closing the error resolves this. If that doesn't work, could you check the logs and see if there is some more info there? The token is not yet valid (iat)  sounds like a time(zone) issue. E.g. our user management and your oasis not running in sync. Your laptop being behind. When you open a new tab, you start a new instance of the gui and it will get a new (potentially out of time) token. So every time you open a new tab there is the potential for this to happen. Could you check your os time settings? 
I am not sure what the Gateway timeout problem is. Is the gateway timeout coming from keycloak or the nomad api?

question: @FAIRmat-internal-areaD Is there any existing plans to have something like the Metainfo Browser that can be launched directly from a plugin? @Nathan Daelman  and I were discussing this morning, and think this would fullfill a long-time desire to be able to browse domain-specific Metainfo structures.?
answer: The normal metainfo browser should show plugins as a "source" when installed. But I guess you want something, where you don't have a nomad installation and just get a browser, e.g.  nomad admin run metainfo-browser ? For install plugins you can use the normal metainfo browser, e.g. here is the  nomad_measurments  on the example oasis: <https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/oasis/gui/analyze/metainfo/nomad_measurements>. Maybe we can improve the metainfo browser, but there is no plan for an external tool/service. I guess eventually we should hightlight the fact that this comes from plugins better. I think we should at least provide a method for printing out all the (inherited) quantities and subsection of a section. I raised this in a comment somewhere earlier.

question:
The error shown in this action https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-schema-plugin-nmr/actions/runs/14413562293/job/40426382597
suggests that the context is not properly created when running the .parse() command in the github pipeline.

Does anyone have hints on how to solve this problem? 
@Sarthak Kapoor  @Lauri Himanen 

Thank you!
FYI @Sathya Sai Seetharaman?
answer:
I think the error points out that the schema definition is not available. I couldn't find any problem with the loading of the schema entry point. One thing I have not seen/used before in schemas is overriding the  init  method as done here: 
https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-schema-plugin-nmr/blob/824fa87a6c395ecf77867f6b89d7a44fb90e095b/src/nomad_nmr_schema/schema_packages/schema_package.py#L370

Maybe this is causing the schema not to be loaded properly. Can you try defining the  init  method as:
 py
def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
  super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)
  self.rank = [3, 3]
 

Also, as you want to only set a property, you could skip using  init  method and make it part of the normalize method.
 py
def normalize(self, archive: 'EntryArchive', logger: 'BoundLogger') -> None:
  super().normalize(archive, logger)
  self.rank = [3, 3] Hi @Sarthak Kapoor and @Andrea Albino, I have dug into the error a bit more. The difference seems to be because I declared a unit for MagneticSusceptibility in the class.

In the original repo as you have created it, the value is declared with unit being 'dimensionless' => https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-schema-plugin-nmr/blob/824fa87a6c395ecf77867f6b89d7a44fb90e095b/src/nomad_nmr_schema/schema_packages/schema_package.py#L362

When I changed the unit to be consistent with literature, the error started popping up. My value definition looks like this now:
value = Quantity(
        type=np.float64,
        unit='10 ** -6 * cm ** 3 / mol',
        description="""
        Value of the macroscopic magnetic susceptibility tensor.
        """,
    )

I tried changing the unit back to *dimensionless* locally and it seemed to fix the error. Any ideas why? My guess is that the way you define the units is not being parsed correctly by pint. Can you test parsing it into pint on python REPL? 

Alternatively, you can compose units using SI base units. This is the convention we usually follow. So for your case it will be “m^3/mol”. The display units can be modified to “cm^3/mol”. if you want to add custom units, i think you will have to first add it to the pint registry When testing on REPL, make sure to use import units registry from nomad.units. I can also test it once i am on my desk in sometime

question:
Hello,
I have an entry with a quantity that can also contain  np.nan  ( dtype float64 ).
When accessing this entry via API with requests everything works as expected.
But when using the ArchiveQuery ( from nomad.client.archive import ArchiveQuery ) the  np.nan  becomes a problem. Because the error  TypeError: Cannot cast array data from dtype('O') to dtype('float64') according to the rule 'safe'   does not let me download the entry.

I was told that a related np.nan problem has been discussed here (https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/issues/2128). Can there also be a solution for the ArchiveQuery?
answer:
There is no way to tell whether a None represents nan or inf, when processing a given dict.
And the typing system does not allow this beahviour due to data safety. What you can do: turn on  as_plain_dict=True  so that download as plain dicts.
Work with plain dicts if possible.
If you need EntryArchives, you need to manually replace affected nones with either nan or inf.
Then call  m_from_dict , reference code can be seen as attached. I forget to mention that, if you have write access to the source code, you can modify this block of code in ArchiveQuery.

There is a hidden flag "treat_none_as_nan", so you can simply change line 476 into:

 py
result = EntryArchive.m_from_dict(
    response_json[index]['archive'], m_context=context, treat_none_as_nan=True
)

I have question regarding testing for workflows. I am currently working on the NEB workflow plugin(https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-neb-workflows) and I am not sure how to test if my scripts are working through the terminal. I am currently always spinning up the GUI which does not feel very efficient. 

For a single computational file I can do  uv run nomad parse neb0.traj > output  but if I try to parse the workflow file directly I get the error  AssertionError: Only archives with upload_id can be referenced  which makes sense. 
I saw a testing.py script from Chema in the neb repo, which looks like it is possible to resolve the references from the workflow through the terminal, but I am not sure how to use it, or how to do something similar. Looking at the testing_AlCo2S4.py file in [tests](https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-neb-workflows/blob/develop/tests/test_AlCo2S4.py), is there a way to run this and inspect an output for the script? Maybe @Alvin Noe Ladines has some tips?
answer:
Hi Julia, you can adap the following script for your testing  from nomad.datamodel import EntryArchive, EntryMetadata
from nomad.datamodel.context import ServerContext
from nomad.files import StagingUploadFiles
from nomad.processing import Upload
from nomad.parsing.parser import ArchiveParser
from electronicparsers.yambo import YamboParser
import sys
from nomad.utils import get_logger

logger = get_logger(__name__)

upload_files = StagingUploadFiles(upload_id='test_upload', create=True)
upload = Upload(upload_id='test_upload')

    m_context=context,
    metadata=EntryMetadata(upload_id=upload.upload_id, entry_id='yambo_entry'),
)

workflow_archive = EntryArchive(
    m_context=context,
    metadata=EntryMetadata(upload_id=upload.upload_id, entry_id='workflow_entry'),
)
ArchiveParser().parse(sys.argv[-2], workflow_archive, logger) the worklow archive needs a context in order to resolve the referenced archive, created an upload test_upload containing an entry yambo_entry which i parse using the yambo parser, i then reference this in a workflow archive something like  {

                "name": "Task",

answer:

question:
Hi, I  get errors when processing data from raw files in a plugin after switching to develop branch (1.3.3.dev142+g56585ed05). Before on NOMAD v1.3.3 the data was processed without errors. NOMAD develop seems to be less "tolerant" with incoming data:
e.g. does not convert "20" into a float:
 
raise ValueError(f'Cannot convert {value} to {self._dtype}.') ValueError: Cannot convert 20 to <class 'numpy.float64'>."
 
I am now adjusting my file reader to explicitly convert the data to e.g. float for example above. But still I was wondering if NOMAD should not convert  '20'  into a float  20.0  as it did before. @Markus Scheidgen?
answer: Maybe a guess, but could be worth a try, add a dot cuz the string "20" otherwise is unclear and maps more to an integer than a float, "20." is a different statement This is due to the new metainfo datatypes merged recently. And yes they are less forgiving now. Auto converting strings into numbers is really a stretch. This conversion from string to float is really the parsers job. @Theodore Chang DM BITTE whats your opinion on this? The type system internally tries to convert the input data into the target type. In this particular case, when a string "20" is given, the type instance tries to check the following: "20" == str(numpy.float64("20")), this results actual check to be something like "20" == "20.000000000000012" due to the inexact nature of floats. The new system tries to be as smart as possible but for cases like this, the risk of loss of precision cannot be avoided. I'll say the easy fix is assign the correct value to the quantity, meaning that instead of "20", use numpy.float64("20").

Hey @yrrliu i usually create new files through parsing and use the normalizer to read the data and move data around. you can create entries in a normalizer the same ways as in a parser., but usually for me at parsing i already know how many files should be created for uploaded data.

what we do for example is we have an excel sheet where each row is the synthesis for one sample with multiple synthesis processes, we then:
1. upload excel sheet
2. check with is_mainfile if it has the right structure
3. iterate over each row and create a sample entry and multiple synthesis processes with the data in the excel sheet
4. link the synthesis processes to the sample (keep in mind if you reference in one direction nomad lets the referenced entry know that it has been linked for queryiing later)

later we then upload measurement data to the sample but this is then file by file based.

This way of building a NOMAD image is not supported anymore, please refer to nomad-distro-template on how to build your own NOMAD oasis image: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-distro-template I assume you could change the source of your  nomad-lab  dependency to point to either a [git repo](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/concepts/projects/dependencies/#git) or [local project](https://docs.astral.sh/uv/concepts/projects/dependencies/#path) In that case, I can recommend cloning the nomad-distro-template repo locally, and pointing nomad-lab to a local project like Haoyu mentioned. question: I'm trying to describe some material properties in a schema. For example, it is mandatory to describe stress properties of the material, etching rates for different techniques and probably others in the future. I was wondering if could be possible to modify the properties section in results (I think this should be the best option but I don't know if as an user I'm allowed to do it). Another possible solution is to add a section to the EntryArchive (i.e. adding a field to data, metadata and results), but I don't know how it is possible. If all these solutions are wrong I will create a subsection in data with the Section ad hoc for properties needed.?
answer: Please put them in the data section The results section is a protected section that can only be extended in the NOMAD source code in the nomad-FAIR repository. With the latest updates you should be able to search and use the quantities in the data section in the same way as those from results so the need for the results section is slowly disappearing. You can add them as searchable quantities in apps: https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/staging/docs/howto/plugins/apps.html#loading-quantity-definitions-into-an-app

question:
Hi all,

does the following code mean that our matrix renderer always swaps rows/cols:

 # gui/src/components/archive/visualizations.js

  let value = shape.length > 1 ? ({rowIndex, columnIndex}) => values[columnIndex][rowIndex] : ({columnIndex}) => values[columnIndex]
  if (invert) {
    value = shape.length > 1 ? ({rowIndex, columnIndex}) => values[rowIndex][columnIndex] : ({rowIndex}) => values[rowIndex]
  } 

 invert  is only applied to 1‑D vectors, so every 2‑D tensor (e.g. 3×3 NMR, stress, dielectric) is displayed transposed compared to the stored archive data. 

To sum up: **why does the table widget transpose the tensor?**

Please also find attached a screenshot from a user?
answer:
@Lauri Himanen or @mohammad.nakhaee could help here In the case of a 1D array, there is no change in how the actual value is accessed. However, for 2D arrays, setting invert effectively transposes the matrix by swapping rowIndex and columnIndex when accessing values. invert only affects how the data is visualized, not the actual stored data. The decision to apply invert is determined by the schema. different theories and methods may represent tensors differently In general I'm really not sure why the UI should transpose the data: I guess it is a matter of how one is used to seeing the data displayed and stored (row-major order and column-major order). and the original person who implemented the visualization (Markus?) made a choice.

I have zero objections in getting rid of the inversion directly in Javascript. Could you Esma create a MR for this and I review? @Lauri Himanen Can you give Esma access to the mpcdf, or instructions on how to get an account/access -- she does not have this yet

question:
Hi NOMAD team,
To have a non editable Quantity in NOMAD there are different ways. One way is to leave out the a_eln part at Quantity definition like:
 python
lab_id = Quantity(
        type=str,
        description="""An ID string that is unique at least for the lab that produced this
            data."""
    )
 
But it appears a bit ugly in the GUI and might be the choise if there is a need for some indexing needed by code or a query and not for to be looked up by the user.

Another way from the documentation should be:
 python
name = Quantity(
        type=str,
        description='A short human readable and descriptive name.',
        a_eln=dict(component='StringEditQuantity', label='name'),
        a_display=dict(editable=False)
    )
 
But that seems not to work. If I try it I am still able to edit.

Finally, I can also do it in the section's m_def:
 python
class MySchemaName(Entity, EntryData):
    m_def = Section(
        a_eln=dict(
            properties=dict(
                editable=dict(exclude=["name", "lab_id"]),
            ),
        ),
    )
 
This works and also can be done with inherited attributes.

Can someone tell me why approach #2 is not working and what is the recommended way to do it?
answer:
From the docs that should indeed work. @mohammad.nakhaee any idea why it doesn't? Hi @Matthias Zelt 

You should be able to use something like:

 python

name = Quantity(
        type=str,
        description='A short human readable and descriptive name.',
        a_eln=dict(component='StringEditQuantity', label='name'),
        a_display=dict(editable={'exclude': 'name'})
    )

 

or more properly:

 python
name = Quantity(
        type=str,
        description='A short human readable and descriptive name.',
        a_eln=dict(component='StringEditQuantity', label='name'),
        a_display=DisplayAnnotation(Filter(exclude='name'))
    ) sorry, I meant the  a_display  should be added to the section definition where the quantity lives DisplayAnnotation is intended for the Section level, not for Quantity. It is as a filter of quantity names.

answer:
Hello, 

Are you trying  docker compose up  inside  ops/docker-compose/infrastructure  folder? It seems to run fine for me The error indicates that you used a newer version of NOMAD and populated data, then switched to an older branch which has the older Elasticsearch version. This causes that error. The brute-force method of solving it is to clear all your local data with  docker compose down --volumes  but note that this will delete all of the stored data (in mongodb, elastic, etc) of your installation.

answer:
Hi@Deleted User! 

So does the lab computer have access to the public internet? If not, then it might just take a long time for the call to  unpkg.com  to fail, causing slow startup. It might be that your local computer already has those resources cached, which would explain why you don't see the error when trying it offline.

The only other that comes to mind is this issue that we had: https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/issues/2065. You should only have this problem if you are running a version < 1.3.4. Could you check your NOMAD version from the GUI information page:  gui/about/information  (scroll to the section "About this distribution") Alright. We don't have that much experience in running the GUI completely offline. The site will try to get a few resources from e.g.  unpkg , which probably ultimately cause the problem on your lab machine. I think this is something that we probably should address. Let me discuss this with the team and we see if we simply get these resources from our server instead.

Why you can't reproduce this locally is not entirely clear to me either. Maybe private browsing does not completely invalidate the cache, or then there is some other caching layer in between. You could try to do a "hard reload" on your local machine while it is offline (on Chrome that is Ctrl+F5). The browser developer tools should also show where the site is getting it's resources under the "network" tab. Yes, I do agree. It could also be that the GUI works mostly just fine even without these libraries, and the issue you have with the loading times is related to something completely different. But yes, Sebastian probably has some insights. Hi, I have no problem using my own keycloak for my Oases at IKZ. I guess I mostly used it from computers that are also connected to the internet.

question:
Is there currently a NOMAD way of saving a experimental measurement uncertainty assigned to a Quantity? (Eg. mean value +- std)
If no, I would imagine this will be useful for everyone handling experimental data.?
answer:
Not yet, but on the PhysicalProperty discussion we are talking about these statistics For now we are adding a second quantity next to it. See for example: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-material-processing/blob/main/src/nomad_material_processing/general.py#L573 My recommendation is: you can try it out now yourself, either following @Hampus Näsström and Area A implementation, or doing it more abstract, following Area C current def of PhysicalProperty (https://github.com/nomad-coe/nomad-simulations/blob/develop/src/nomad_simulations/schema_packages/physical_property.py#L69). Bear in mind: 1. the proper implementation of  PhysicalProperty  might take some weeks/months still, 2. whatever you implement now it should be then refactor afterwards into this new structure.

I'd say 2nd option is more appealing from my point of view, as it will directly reflect on implementing something practical on the interoperability TF, and it will allow you to be ready to refactor more consistently and easy for you. First option might mean you need to change a lot of logic here and there.

I think @Markus Scheidgen had some slides with a suggestion for statistics under a PhysicalProperty Note that experimental datasets in NOMAD parsed from NeXus are following the NeXus convention (similar to what Hampus showed):
Errors or uncertainties on a data quantity DATA__field are stored in the DATA_errors__field
You can see it in the Ellipsometry example upload how it is represented in NeXus and also in NOMAD.

question:
I have the problem that my test uploads are not displayed anymore in my local test oasis, despite being present in the corresponding ".volumes" folder on the disk.
This happened after I started another test oasis in a different directory. For this purpose I deleted all the docker volumes (but not the files), so that the old uploads are not displayed in my new test oasis anymore. Now I wanted to check the old test oasis again and thought, that I would still be able to see the old uploads there, because they are still in the volumes folder. But they are not displayed. Is this a logic error from my side or does someone know how to fix this? Thanks!?
answer:
Hi @Aaron Schüller-Ruhl!

The docker volumes contain all the information that we store in databases and in our search index. Without this information the raw data and the archives stored in the  .volumes  folder data will not be available.

It is still possible to somewhat recover your data by using the raw data. If you don't have thousands of uploads, the simplest method is to move the  fs  folder out of the  .volumes  folder and then re-upload them individually. They will then get reprocessed and assigned new identifiers. Note that the  staging  folder contains unpublished uploads, and the  public  folder contains published ones.

There are also programmatic ways to achieve something similar, so if you wish to recover a lot of data, ask me for further instructions. In the future, if you wish to make a backup of your data so that you are easily able to fully recover it, follow the guide here: https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/staging/docs/howto/oasis/admin.html#backups Restoring data with the same  upload_id  (or other ids like  entry_id ) is quite tricky.

@Theodore Chang DM BITTE: Is it possible to provide fixed ids using the bundle format? Can upload bundles be generated easily just from the data stored in  .volumes ?

The final brute-force alternative is to modify the values directly in MongoDB and in the produced archives. It is possible, but needs quite a bit of work. Bundles also pull data from mongo, solely from .volumes is not possible. Upload id is an random id, cannot fix it. Entry id is a deterministic id based on upload id and file name so as long as upload id is the same, entry id shall not change.

question:
in our plugins the parsers look for samples bei  results.eln.lab_ids  and links them. When writing tests for that parsers i get this error:
 nomad.search.QueryValidationError: results.eln.lab_ids is not a doc quantity   why is it not a doc quantity? i also get annother validation error:  nomad.search.QueryValidationError: upload_id is not a doc quantity?
answer:
So during your test you are doing an API call or using the  nomad.search  module I guess? I don't think that by default any of the search infrastructure is setup for tests, as typically you only work with the data contained in the archive itself. That is probably why you are getting that error. But I could probably say more if I see the test code that you are trying to run. I think the safest and fastest choice for tests is to mock some of the API/search calls. You can learn more about mocking with pytest here: https://docs.pytest.org/en/stable/how-to/monkeypatch.html

The other alternative is that in your test pipelines you really do setup the whole required infrastructure, but that might get tricky.

question:
When running nomad installation with app_worker, the  m_context  is an instance of the class  nomad.datamodel.context.ServerContext  which has a well-defined  raw_path_exists  method. But while running pytests, which uses the nomad as a package (and not the local installation),  m_context  is an instance of class  nomad.datamodel.context.Context   in which  raw_path_exists  is not implemented.

Because of this difference, the pytests fail during the plugin testing, when using  archive.m_context.raw_path_exists(file_path) . Can we harmonize these two methods for the classes  ServerContext  and  Context ? Clarification: when using pytest,  m_context  is of class  nomad.datamodel.context.ClientContext  which inherits  nomad.datamode.context.Context , and as the child doesn't have its own  raw_path_exists  method, it defaults to the one from the parent, which is not implemented.?
answer: I guess we only implemented the Context interface based on what was necessary. The ServerContext is the more complete one as this is used all the time in the app. The ClientContext is only used by, well, clients. E.g. when you do the "nomad parse" command, or the ArchiveQuery instantiates metainfo object froms the retrieved data. For the first case "nomad parse" you commit seems ok. For the second one, it does not hurt, but it also does not help, as the raw data is most likely not locally available. I guess it complements the existing  raw...  functions. Feel free to create an MR and merge this.

question:
Dear developers,

I have some questions regarding publishing data from a NOMAD-oasis.

We have:

    a local NOMAD-oasis installation
    different simulation and measurement data-set for magnetic materials which are read via our own developed parsers (many different file formats like txt, json, hdf5 (nexus, non-nexus), ...)


We need: 

    to publish our datasets on central NOMAD.
    Is it possible to publish on the oasis without moving data to the central one?
    possibility to test the publishing process.


How is this currently possible (Publish it on local NOMAD than publishing on/to central NOMAD)?

The NOMAD-documentation only states (https://nomad-lab.eu/nomad-lab/nomad-oasis.html):
"Publish selected data: NOMAD Oasis will soon be connected to the public NOMAD service, allowing you to publish selected data with one click."

Can one please point me into the right direction. I/we are also open for online meetings.
Thanks in advance,
BR
Wilfried Crosslink to a related discussion: "Publishing an entry from Oasis to Central NOMAD" https://discord.com/channels/1201445470485106719/1278619735675633725?
answer:
Hi @Wilfried_Hortschitz, We are having a bit of a intense time on our side due to the preparation of some important meetings and deadlines coming on our side. Sorry if our responses come late. 

At the moment, the feature of shipping data from an Oasis with custom plugins to the central NOMAD is not fully covered, meaning that the schemas will not be recognized as those plugins might not be installed in our central sevrice. It is in our agenda to cover this, but we are not fully there. We have to have in mind that there will not be an immediate solution as this can get really hard and is nothing that we can get done in the next few weeks. But it really is in our agenda! 

I believe we have our first cases of publishing comp data from an Oasis to the central NOMAD, but in this case, both installations were sharing the same plugin relevant for that data. Perhaps @Lauri Himanen will be able to comment more about the progress. 

Regarding the question about to publish in an Oasis. Yes, you can do that (on your own risks and rules), but to generate a DOI in those datasets you will need to configure your NOMAD installation to have a DOI provider (https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/docs/reference/config.html#datacite). Also, I guess you will need to have your Oasis openly available (not only on your intranet).  

I am sorry if the answers from the relevant experts these days do not come super fast. Busy days! 🙏

question: @Bernadette Mohr and I both experienced the dev deployment is hanging upon upload. Is this a known issue @Markus Scheidgen @Lauri Himanen ?
answer:
Can you tell a bit more? Do you mean the  develop  deployment (https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/develop/gui) or your local dev installation? I have two hanging upload attempts, they get to "Upload is processing ... Waiting for results (level 0), and then they hang for all eternity. At least these two do. I started the same upload again, it still took quite long but it finally passed. No idea what was going on between Friday  and this morning. Typically when an upload hangs it is because of resources: the processing e.g. runs out of memory or crashes in some other unexpected reason. If possible, this should then be debugged locally. Also the  develop -deployment is not really equitpped with very beefy machines, so trying to process anything very large there can be problematic.

@Joseph Rudzinski: Are these all uploads that are left in a limbo state because of failed processing? They are not published or anything?

question: HI, there is some documentation already on the use of the H5WebAnnotation to add axes labels and units to h5 quantities. https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/staging/docs/howto/customization/hdf5.html?
answer:
Dear @Amir Golparvar ,
I need to upload .h5 (hdf5) files for publishing on central NOMAD. If I understand it correctly from the documentation (https://nomad-lab.eu/prod/v1/staging/docs/howto/customization/basics.html#hdf5normalizer) this should be possible via using custom schemas. Therefore, I created some really basic example with which I wanted to test it:
I created a 'Js.h5' files with only one entry/dataset in it. This files was created using h5py and the following code:
 
import h5py
hdf5_file = "Js.h5"
with h5py.File(hdf5_file, "w") as h5f:
    # Store Js directly at the root
    js_dataset = h5f.create_dataset("Js", data=123.456)
 
The output in Js.h5 looks in HDF5View as in the attached screenshot.
Based on the above linked documentation I created a custom schema for uploading the Js.h5 which looks like:
 
definitions:
  name: 'hdf5'
  sections:
    Test_HDF5:
      base_sections:
        - 'nomad.datamodel.data.EntryData'
        - 'nomad.datamodel.metainfo.basesections.HDF5Normalizer'
      quantities:
        datafile:
          type: str
          m_annotations:
            eln:
              component: FileEditQuantity
        Js:
          type: np.float64
          shape: [ '*' ]
          m_annotations:
            hdf5:
              path: '/Js' 
 
But finally I am not able to get the value of Js populated into the 'processded_data->data->Js' sub-section as you can see in the second screenshot "upload with custom-shema in central-NOMAD". 
What am I doing wrong?
Is this  problem related to the path? I found a more specific example on https://github.com/nomad-coe/nomad/tree/d0e17625acbfc7f144fd6eb0a11cca79677fe7bb/tests/data/datamodel/metainfo/eln/h5 where the .h5 data file is also included. This example is working on Central-NOMAD. @Amir Golparvar 
Why does the processing of this 1.7MB example .h5 file take several minutes?
This is the same for a sample file of mine with 2.4kB.
I also get alway a "Unexpected error: "[object Object] (500)". Please try again and let us know, if this error keeps happening." in a red bar in the web-interface when uploading the schema + .h5 file Hi Wilfried,

This is actually a bug, the normalizer expects a list and not a single value. I am fixing it now

meanwhile, this is working right now, not sure if its useful for you:

 python
import h5py
hdf5_file = "Js.h5"
with h5py.File(hdf5_file, "w") as h5f:
    # Store Js directly at the root
    js_dataset = h5f.create_dataset("Js", data=[123.456])

My message is with regards to this PR - https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-schema-plugin-nmr/pull/1, where I am trying to add in some physical quantities validation to the tests to present example representations of each data class that the schema package defines. I have tried to build upon the test_schema_package.py file within the tests folder. I'm attaching the tests folder that I'm locally working on as a .zip file.

The parse function from nomad.client packages the chemical symbol and index information in my input test file as a MProxy object, but the normalizer expects an AtomsState object for the data to be ingested correctly. Could you help me improve the test code and/or my example files please? This is the only outstanding issue before I can close the PR. answer:
Hi Sathya, when sections are added as reference, they are composed with  MProxy  definition, which has a method to resolve the reference and replace  MProxy  with the actual section definition ( AtomsState  in your case). Can you try the following:
 py
from nomad.metainfo import MProxy
..

atom_state = <path to the instance>
if isinstance(atom_state, MProxy):
  atom_state.m_proxy_resolve()

# now access the quantity in the object
assert atom_state.my_quantity Hi Sathya, i have added a  label  quantity under  AtomsState , and please see my comment in the issue👌

  - nomad.service: worker
  - nomad.version: 1.3.16.dev170+g5f02437e9

We obtained these warnings in the worker at the beginning but successively the application actually started. So what could the problem be?
answer:
That error means the  app  is not running yet, when the app is running the output of  uv run poe start  should show the following output:

 
INFO:     Started server process [4179]
INFO:     Waiting for application startup.
INFO:     Application startup complete.
INFO:     Uvicorn running on http://0.0.0.0:8000 (Press CTRL+C to quit) Hi @Trogg!

Maybe you could clarify a few points:
 - You mentioned that you are here using  nomad-distro-dev , is this correct?
 - You also mention that "We are installing the nomad distribution from a server of our institution and than trying to access from the local browser of  our PC.". Do I understand correctly that you run  nomad-distro-dev  on some institution server, and then try to access it from another computer over the network? This sounds like a use case for which  nomad-distro-dev  is not primarily designed for: you would have to be quite careful in opening the correct ports etc.  nomad-distro-dev  is primarily designed all to be run from a single computer and accessed only from that same computer as it does not have a proxy that could redirect the traffic correctly.

With these clarifications we might be able to help you further. I am using that same use case (headless server for nomad-distro-dev), and added a note to the readme about the required port forwardings: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-distro-dev/blob/21a1566fc1d8ced3757d2d2b60ecea64f24f4b58/README.md?plain=1#L311 But I don't think that this relates to your problem

question:
After following the last tutorial on the installation of an Oasis using the Github repo to create a Docker image of an Oasis with plugin code installed, I am trying to make it work on my machine by developing a parser for the DAMASK code.
Since I am not familiar with the coding convention of NOMAD, I make a lot of changes to test the output visible on the Oasis I am creating and so it means that, following the steps of the tutorial, once I make a modification on the parser code, I generate a new Docker image that I want to test.
So each time I want to test my new Oasis image, I do  sudo rm -r nomad-oasis  with  nomad-oasis  being the folder containing the  docker-compose.yaml  file and the  configs  folder and I also delete the Docker image with  docker rmi <id-of-the-image>  and then I  curl  the new image and proceed to the steps of the installation.
However, when I open the Oasis in my browser, I can see the entries but when I click on one of them (each of them), I have this message:
 
Unexpected error: "[object Object] (500)". Please try again and let us know, if this error keeps happening.
 
And the  Files  panel says  ERROR Could not initialize view .
This happens whether  the entries are published or just uploaded, so for now, it means that I can't publish any dataset because when I will install a new modified image, I won't be able to properly access the entry and so I have to republish them.

Is this a normal behaviour or am I doing something wrong when I am trying to visualize the modifications I made on my image by deleting the repo and the Docker image systematically ?
answer:
Hi Guillaume! You should not need to delete anything when updating the image. Taken directly from the image README:
"Whenever you update your image you need to shut down NOMAD using

 docker compose down 

and then repeat steps 5. and 6. above." With steps 5. and 6. being: 
"
5.  Pull the images specified in the docker-compose.yaml (retrieved from the nomad-oasis.zip). Note that the image needs to be public or you need to provide a PAT (see "Important" note above).

 docker compose pull 

6.  And run it with docker compose in detached (--detach or -d) mode

 docker compose up -d 
" When you do the delete command from above you are deleting the files folder which contains all of the entries so that is why NOMAD cannot find them. That's a bit above my knowledge of the NOMAD backend but my guess would be that the Mongo DB index somehow survived in it's own docker image while the files it's referencing to were deleted. @Markus Scheidgen might be able to tell you more.

question:
Dear all,

I've been struggling in the last few days with updating my local oasis to latest nomad + having some local patches on top. I already received some help from @ahmed ilyas which I'm really grateful for, however I think I did not make my intentions super clear originally, so I'll try to describe in more detail what is my setup.

What I'm trying to do some machine learning based on the upstream NOMAD data. I want to combine results from several DFT codes and I'm really happy with NOMAD here since the metainfo and parser support are great to mostly enable this. Specifically, what I need are some results, like structures, energies, or forces, but also quite detailed info about the method (used exchange-correlation functional, whether some more advanced stuff was used, like vdW, +U, charged cell, spin-polarization, spin-orbit,  etc.) and numerical settings (basis set size, k-point density, convergence, ...) to be able to create a consistent dataset with reasonable accuracy. There are some missing pieces so I need to make some local changes to parsers and also nomad-lab itself (datamodel, normalizing, etc.) deploy it locally in my oasis (running in Kubernetes cluster of my university), download the relevant raw data from upstream (approx. tens of thousands of entries) and reprocess it using my custom setup. The recommended way to customize Oasis is nomad-distro-template, which is simple and allows to customize the plugins. I cannot however customize nomad-lab itself (unless I actually manage to build a local package of it first with the GUI, which I did not yet), so this is limited to a release version only (while some of the local patches are probably suitable for upstream, some probably don't make sense beyond my custom oasis and in general to be able to work efficiently I can't wait for a release to be able to update my oasis. 

I was also suggested nomad-distro-dev which allows to setup a really custom environment and submodules are something I'm already familiar with. But I can't build a docker image from this, so not really suitable for running of oasis in K8s.

I'm aware that not everything I'm doing is supported (custom nomad-lab patches) or K8s nomad installations. However from my point of view this is a regression, since in the past it was possible to have a single working tree with submodules, install it locally, run quick testing with bare-bone linux while still being able to build a docker image when needed using the exact same environment.

Any advice would be welcomed.?
answer:
Thanks for the detailed issue. I can try to answer some of the technical stuff, the physics stuff is a bit beyond my scope but @Sarthak Kapoor might find the ML use case interesting and see if NOMAD actions might be a fit. This is a work in progress, but you can take a look at the docs and see if this could be something that might work here: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/nomad-docs/pull/52 



This is a limitation by design. In order for the plugin system to function reliably and allow plugins to interoperate across different installations, the base  nomad-lab  must remain compatible and consistent.

In the past, we encountered significant issues with customized nomad versions. Certain plugins would work in one setup but break in another.

Potential solutions to revert back previous behaviour: 
1. In your nomad-lab branch, bring back the Dockerfile final stages from before and build it locally the previous way.
2. Create a fork of nomad-lab and release a package under a different name and make nomad-distro-template install this custom package instead of nomad-lab. 

Neither of the solutions are optimal but it could work. Hi @Pavel Ondračka, your use case is quite interesting. Do you plan to use NOMAD only for data aggregation or also to run training/inference? We are building a feature that allows triggering long-running tasks in NOMAD, independently from the entry processing, and provides quite some freedom in what resources you wanna allocate for these tasks. Would be happy to share more if you feel this will be useful 🙂

question: Are there some updates here? is it possible to get a registered doi on an oasis?
answer: Here's another dead DOI link from @Luis Walter: https://dx.doi.org/10.17172/NOMAD/2024.10.01-1 The "DOIs from Oasis" is a different topic. To create a DOI, you need an account with datacite (or another company that does this) to use their APIs to create DOIs. There are fees associated etc. We only have this for the central NOMAD. I don't think, its a good idea or even legally allowed to share the account credentials with Oases. I now fixed the dois:  https://dx.doi.org/10.17172/NOMAD/2024.10.01-1, https://dx.doi.org/10.17172/NOMAD/2024.09.25-1,  https://dx.doi.org/10.17172/NOMAD/2024.09.25-2 You are right about the disable, we should disable (or not even show) the "Assign DOI" button, if the config has  datacite.enabled: false  (which is the default).

question: Currently, the memory on many of the oases gets blocked over time and the only way to free it is to restart the containers. @Markus Scheidgen mentioned to me at some point that this is due to the workers being reused so if there is some memory leak in them they block the memory indefinitely. He did mention that there was a way to limit the reuse and/or number of workers somehow to mitigate this. As it's Markus last day today I guess he won't be able to answer but maybe @Lauri Himanen has an idea who could? Thanks!?
answer: Probably this - https://docs.celeryq.dev/en/stable/userguide/workers.html#max-memory-per-child-setting By default it's the number of cores https://docs.celeryq.dev/en/stable/userguide/workers.html#concurrency python -c "import multiprocessing; print(multiprocessing.cpu_count())"  should give you the right number I guess the max memory per child has a similar effdect. But, there is another important setting. This is the one i was mentioning once. This one specifically mentiones the use case of memory leaks: https://docs.celeryq.dev/en/latest/userguide/workers.html#max-tasks-per-child-setting

question:
Hey, I wanted to use a SectionProxy, becasue I reference a Section before defining it in the same python script.
In the section UMR_InternalSubstrate  I have the following quantity:

 standard_substrate_lot = Quantity( 
         type=SectionProxy('UMR_StandardSubstrateLot'), 
         a_eln=dict(component='ReferenceEditQuantity')) 

And in the same script below I define the Section:
 class UMR_StandardSubstrateLot(EntryData):  
     m_def = Section() 

But the App Container is unhealthy and I get the following error:
 nomad.metainfo.metainfo.MetainfoError: Type UMR_schemas.substrate.UMR_StandardSubstrateLot:Section of nomad.metainfo.metainfo.Quantity.type:Quantity is not a valid metainfo quantity type 

Any ideas what I am missing out here? This are the full logs of the App Container:?
answer:
Define UMR_StandardSubstrateLot first. If I understood it correctly the SectionProxy is used if you define a section with the section class itself. For example if you have a section Process Step which can have Substeps with the same attributes you can do:
 python
class ProcessStep(ArchiveSection):
    m_def = Section(label_quantity='name')

    name = Quantity(
        type=str,
        a_eln=dict(
            component='StringEditQuantity'))

    steps = SubSection(
        section_def=SectionProxy('ProcessStep'), repeats=True)

nomad.metainfo.metainfo.MetainfoReferenceError: could not resolve nexus.NeXus here is the archive what it tries to read back:?
answer:
The module path is no more 'nexus' so it cannot be found by resolver. One shall change the package name from 'nexus' to 'pynxtools.nomad.schema', which is the correct module path where the package resides in. Maybe: https://github.com/FAIRmat-NFDI/pynxtools/blob/ad263939f31d32aa676dc395bd2e36b551905280/src/pynxtools/nomad/schema.py#L115

Maybe other places as well. The first MetainfoReferenceError is caused by the wrong module name, which is used to resolve the definition.

question:
Yesterday, I decided to upgrade my repository that create the Docker image for my Oasis. I followed the changes and included my plugins in the  .toml  file and it works perfectly on my personal Github account.
However, in the AddMorePower project, we created a github account to centralise all the developments in the project and I am now using this project repository to generate the Oasis image. With the previous way of doing it (with the  plugins.txt  file), it was working correctly. But I decided to switch to the new system, as it was working correctly on my personal account but this time, I have some troubles when I run the workflow  Build and publish docker images ...
In this workflow, the  Update Python Lock file  and  Build Docker Image (jupyter)  are passing but the  Build Docker Image (app)  is failing with this error:
 
#27 exporting to image
#27 pushing layers 0.6s done
#27 ERROR: failed to push ghcr.io/addmorepower/nomad_oasis_image:main: unexpected status from HEAD request to https://ghcr.io/v2/addmorepower/nomad_oasis_image/blobs/sha256:41fb72fc6d430c438822a92a7d97d507f4dcef2cb28b3f0c5efccf1b38906ef7: 403 Forbidden

 > exporting to image:
------
ERROR: failed to solve: failed to push ghcr.io/addmorepower/nomad_oasis_image:main: unexpected status from HEAD request to https://ghcr.io/v2/addmorepower/nomad_oasis_image/blobs/sha256:41fb72fc6d430c438822a92a7d97d507f4dcef2cb28b3f0c5efccf1b38906ef7: 403 Forbidden
Error: buildx failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: failed to push ghcr.io/addmorepower/nomad_oasis_image:main: unexpected status from HEAD request to https://ghcr.io/v2/addmorepower/nomad_oasis_image/blobs/sha256:41fb72fc6d430c438822a92a7d97d507f4dcef2cb28b3f0c5efccf1b38906ef7: 403 Forbidden
 
I searched for this error online and some solutions found (Set to  Read and write permissions  in the "Workflow permissions" section of "Settings" -> "Actions" -> "General" for instance) but nothing works...

I can see the file preview, with the nice rendering of the hdf5 (nexus) file, but no processing no log, nothing. It's not crashed it's just that nothing happens.

Details:
version: 1.3.2.dev5+gf9fea27e7
plugin packages: nomad_hysprint,
parsers: nomad_hysprint.parsers:hysprint_parser,
schema packages: nomad_hysprint.schema_packages:hysprint_package,

On the other hand if I upload of my other oasis with normal nomad installation it works well, files are processed, I got a few warnings in the log, nothing special.

But now, I realize my other oasis has loads of parsers. So that may be the reason. can I just get those parsers installed while still having the plugin, or can I use a different image for installation?  Or yeah... in short: does anyone have experience with that, and what can I do?

question:
I think this interests you, @Markus Scheidgen @Theodore Chang DM BITTE

Apparently, there are issues sometimes when defining classes or quantities with the same name. It happened to me when dealing with the  GW(ExcitedStateMethodology)  and  BSE(ExcitedStateMethodology)  method classes, both had a  type  quantity and it was a problem when using  m_copy  in results (check [these lines in the schema](https://gitlab.mpcdf.mpg.de/nomad-lab/nomad-FAIR/-/blob/develop/nomad/datamodel/results.py?ref_type=heads#L1944), and how I had to patch it).

Now I think I have a similar issue. There are two classes and they are in conflict:

1. one defined in the  schema/simulation/run , called  Energy(MSection)  (check [this](https://github.com/nomad-coe/nomad-schema-plugin-run/blob/develop/runschema/calculation.py#L338)),
2. and another new one I am defining in the  schema/simulation/data , called  Energy(Variables)  (check [this](https://github.com/nomad-coe/nomad-schema-plugin-simulation-data/blob/develop/src/nomad_simulations/variables.py#L112)). 

When a Normalizer (especifically, the [DOSNormalizer](https://github.com/nomad-coe/nomad-normalizer-plugin-dos/blob/develop/dosnormalizer/normalizer.py#L57)) runs for the  run.energy  section, instead of considering the point 1 section, it is considering that it is the one defined in point 2, and hence it is missing some attributes and raising an AttributeError when doing  MSection.__getattr__  in the normalizer for some specific quantity.

This is a very weird error, and I don't know how to solve it. @Markus Scheidgen do you want me to open an issue in Gitlab? It would be nice if this gets some priority, as it might become very annoying now that I am trying to transfer  Normalizer  into the  normalize()  function of classes.

Thanks 🙂 (I can share a branch with the refs to the corresponding submodules updated and where I realized this complex error, just let me know).?
answer: If you have a branch and some instruction (e.g.  nomad parser test/data... ) on how to reproduce this, we can try to debug it. Can you bump @Theodore Chang DM BITTE  with a DM.
